name: Medicine
description: Medical terminology, diseases, discoveries, and treatments

questions.q: What is the medical term for hiccups?

source.notes: Caused by involuntary diaphragm contractions.

questions.q: Cephalalgia is the medical term for what?

source.notes: From Greek 'kephale' (head) + 'algos' (pain).

questions.q: Who discovered the smallpox vaccination in 1796?

source.notes: English doctor. Noticed milkmaids with cowpox were immune to smallpox.

questions.q: Who discovered penicillin in 1928?

source.notes: Scottish physician. Discovered by accident when mold contaminated a bacterial culture.

questions.q: Jonas Salk is famous for developing a vaccine for which disease in the 1950s?

source.notes: Field tested 1954. Licensed 1955. Salk refused to patent it — 'Could you patent the sun?'

questions.q: Bovinophobia is the fear of what?

source.notes: From Latin 'bos/bovis' (ox, cow).

questions.q: Small brown spots on the skin, often on the face, are medically known as lentigines. What are they commonly called?

source.notes: Caused by increased melanin production in response to UV light.

questions.q: How many chambers are in the human heart?

source.notes: Two atria (upper) and two ventricles (lower).

questions.q: What is the body's longest nerve, running from the lower back down each leg?

source.notes: Can be as thick as a finger. Compression causes sciatica pain.

questions.q: Which hormone regulates blood sugar by enabling cells to absorb glucose?

source.notes: Produced by the pancreas. Diabetics have insulin deficiency or resistance.

questions.q: What disease is caused by the lack of vitamin C?

source.notes: Historically plagued sailors. Limes/citrus given to British sailors — thus 'limeys.'

questions.q: What gland sits in the neck and regulates metabolism?

source.notes: Butterfly-shaped. Produces T3 and T4 hormones.

questions.q: Appendicitis is inflammation of which organ?

source.notes: Small pouch near the start of the large intestine. Affects ~7% of people in their lifetime.

questions.q: Which part of the eye contains the photoreceptor cells (rods and cones)?

source.notes: Light-sensitive layer at the back of the eye. Rods detect light; cones detect color.

questions.q: What are the two main types of diabetes called?

source.notes: Type 1: autoimmune, no insulin production. Type 2: insulin resistance.

questions.q: What is the medical term for high blood pressure?

source.notes: A major risk factor for heart disease and stroke.

questions.q: Who developed the theory of germ-caused disease and the process of 'pasteurization'?

source.notes: French microbiologist. Also developed the rabies vaccine.

questions.q: The COVID-19 virus is a type of what?

source.notes: Officially SARS-CoV-2. First identified December 2019 in Wuhan, China.

questions.q: Which disease is often referred to as 'consumption' in historical contexts?

source.notes: Called 'consumption' because it seemed to consume the body. Killed millions in the 19th century.

questions.q: What is the name of the electronic device that records heart electrical activity?

source.notes: Electrocardiogram. EEG measures brain activity instead.
